title: cocculus mollis
intro: Cocculus mollis, also known as the Chinese Argyreia or Devil's Claw Vine, is a woody climber native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Cocculus mollis is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or extract, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
